name: Kueue
tagline: Kubernetes-native job queueing with quotas and admission control for batch and ML workloads
seoTitle: Kueue: Kubernetes Job Queueing & Quota Manager
metaDescription: Kueue is a Kubernetes-native job queueing system that controls quotas and admission for batch and ML training jobs, so workloads start only when capacity is free.
overview: Kueue is a set of APIs and a controller for job queueing on Kubernetes. It is a job-level manager that decides when a job should be admitted to start (when its pods can be created) and when it should be stopped (when its active pods should be deleted), based on the quotas you define.

It is built for platform and MLOps teams who run batch and ML training jobs on shared clusters and need to share a fixed pool of CPU, memory, and GPUs fairly across teams. Instead of letting every job create pods immediately, jobs wait in a queue and are admitted only when quota is available.

Within compute orchestration, Kueue sits above the Kubernetes scheduler rather than replacing it. It has built-in support for common job types, including the batch Job, Kubeflow training jobs, RayJob, RayCluster, JobSet, and plain Pods and Pod groups.
features: Job queueing with StrictFIFO and BestEffortFIFO strategies and priority-based ordering
Resource management with resource flavors, cohorts, fair sharing, and preemption policies between tenants
Built-in integrations for BatchJob, Kubeflow training jobs, RayJob, RayCluster, JobSet, and plain Pods and Pod groups
AdmissionChecks let internal or external components influence whether a workload is admitted, including cluster-autoscaler provisioningRequest
Partial admission and dynamic reclaim run jobs with reduced parallelism and release quota as pods complete
MultiKueue multi-cluster dispatch and topology-aware scheduling for pod-to-pod communication throughput

gettingStarted.intro: Install the Kueue controller into an existing Kubernetes cluster, then opt a job into queueing by adding a queue-name label.

steps.heading: Install Kueue
steps.body: Apply the released manifests with server-side apply. Requires Kubernetes 1.29 or newer.

steps.heading: Configure quotas
steps.body: Create a ResourceFlavor, a ClusterQueue that holds the quota, and a LocalQueue in your namespace. The official quickstart documents these objects; the queue name used below (user-queue) must match a LocalQueue you create.

steps.heading: Submit a job to the queue
steps.body: Add the kueue.x-k8s.io/queue-name label to a Job so Kueue manages its admission, then create it.
steps.code:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  generateName: sample-job-
  namespace: default
  labels:
    kueue.x-k8s.io/queue-name: user-queue
spec:
  parallelism: 3
  completions: 3
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: dummy-job
        image: registry.k8s.io/e2e-test-images/agnhost:2.53
        command: [ "/bin/sh" ]
        args: [ "-c", "sleep 60" ]
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "1"
            memory: "200Mi"
      restartPolicy: Never

steps.heading: Create the job
steps.body: Kueue creates a Workload object for the job and admits it once quota is available.

useCases: Share a fixed pool of GPUs across several teams with per-team quotas and fair sharing on one cluster
Queue large batch or ML training jobs so they start only when capacity frees up instead of failing to schedule
Run Kubeflow, Ray, or JobSet training jobs under quota and admission control without changing the scheduler
Dispatch work across multiple clusters with MultiKueue to find spare capacity and off-load the main cluster
